kernel_5op.intake: investigate ROOT cause of 76 UNKNOWN_TIMEOUT tools from #24 (hypothesized 'import-time env probes')

F1_76_tool_list_extracted_from_audit_wit.method: json walk over per_tool_element_matrix where status==UNKNOWN_TIMEOUT

F2_5_to_10_sample_investigated.method: per-tool /usr/bin/time -p with HEXA_ARGV=--help, 10s timeout, stdin=/dev/null, fresh container

F3_cause_classification_honest_evidence_.evidence_artifact: docker exec hexa-exec sh -c 'echo HEXA_ARGV=[$HEXA_ARGV]' returned EMPTY despite caller setting HEXA_ARGV=--help
F3_cause_classification_honest_evidence_.shim_line_106: exec docker exec -i -w PWD -e HEXA_RESOLVER_NO_REROUTE=1 -e HEXA_RESOLVER_IN_CONTAINER=1 hexa-exec /usr/local/bin/hexa "$@"  # MISSING: -e HEXA_ARGV
secondary_causes: NO_HELP_GATE in tool source (e.g. cli_budget_gate has zero --help branch)
FORK_STORM in container — accumulated zombie sh/timeout procs from prior runs hit 504/512 PID ulimit, blocked all new exec
FILE_IO before --help branch (none observed in sample — every tool that DID gate --help still hung because HEXA_ARGV never reached interp)

F4_fast_path_pattern_specd.two_layer: (a) shim layer: forward HEXA_ARGV; (b) tool layer: --help gate FIRST line of main, BEFORE any exec/file/network call

F5_not_paint_over_of_3s_timeout_as_accep.stance: 3s timeout is NOT the bug — it is a symptom. The actual bug is (1) shim drops HEXA_ARGV so --help never reaches script, and (2) several scripts have NO --help gate at all and run full pipeline.
F5_not_paint_over_of_3s_timeout_as_accep.honesty_check: REJECT 'just raise audit timeout' as fix. The audit caught a real defect class.

cause_investigation.method: fresh-container per-tool timing + docker top during hang + source code audit
cause_investigation.key_finding: shim env-leak is the multiplier; tool-level NO_HELP_GATE is the substrate

fast_path_design.method: compare to atlas_shard_scope_lint (passes #24 element check), generalize to a one-liner pattern

axes_2.correlation_check: axes orthogonal: shim fix is one-line patch in hexa_interp shim; tool fix is per-script. Independent.

sample_table.subcause: full apply pipeline runs; killed by 10s timeout
treats --help as unknown subcommand → emits usage to stderr, exits 2. Misclassified by #24 as TIMEOUT only because shim dropped ARGV; tool itself responds quickly.
emits witness file (atlas append) — SIDE_EFFECT_ON_HELP violation per kick instructions
Source DOES gate --help (line 173) but shim drops HEXA_ARGV so script enters default-roots find over 4 huge trees (~/.hx, nexus, hexa-lang, anima). docker top confirmed live `find` child PID at hang.
sample_table.evidence: docker top showed PID 6211: sh -c find /Users/ghost/core/nexus ...

sample_table.subcause: main() unconditionally scans ~/.claude-claude10/projects/*.jsonl, acquires file lock, emits warning JSON, spawns prompt-gen subprocess. Severe SIDE_EFFECT_ON_HELP.
attempts NIST physics.nist.gov fetch via curl; fast only because fetch fails. With network, would block on TCP timeout.
walks falsifier registry, computes shasum -a 256 chain hashes via repeated `printf | shasum` exec — N forks per entry
scans 4 design/ subdirs, emits kick tree — useful but unconditional

sample_table.stderr_first: REJECT: projects.json 에 nexus.loop 섹션 없음
sample_table.subcause: reads projects.json then rejects — no help branch
scans git repos for recent commits — full pipeline runs

PRIMARY_SHIM_LEAK.evidence: every sample tool received empty HEXA_ARGV inside container
PRIMARY_SHIM_LEAK.fix_locus: /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/build/hexa_interp line 106 — add `-e HEXA_ARGV` to docker exec args

NO_HELP_GATE.evidence: source inspection — no `if want_help` branch, or branch placed AFTER side-effects

FILE_IO_BEFORE_HELP_CHECK.note: bak_audit DOES have help branch but shim leak causes it to never trigger

NETWORK_BEFORE_HELP_CHECK.note: would block 30s+ if NIST endpoint timed out instead of failing fast

tools: cli_budget_gate (warning emit + subprocess spawn + disk write)

CONTAINER_FORK_STORM_AMPLIFIER.tools: all 76 once container hits PID ulimit 512
CONTAINER_FORK_STORM_AMPLIFIER.evidence: container at 511/512 PIDs after ~30 cumulative test invocations; all subsequent exec returned 'sh: can't fork: Resource temporarily unavailable'
CONTAINER_FORK_STORM_AMPLIFIER.note: this is SECONDARY — masks underlying issue but appears as 'random hangs'

fast_path_pattern.name: two-layer minimum --help fast-path
fast_path_pattern.rationale: 3s --help cap is reasonable for CLI ergonomics (POSIX precedent: every standard tool emits --help in <100ms). The 76 timeouts are real defects.

layer_a_shim_fix.delta: single `-e HEXA_ARGV` flag (no value = pass-through current value)
layer_a_shim_fix.blast_radius: AUDIT_ONLY this kick — patch belongs to a separate hexa-lang-side kick. raw 9 hexa-only-suffix forbids editing shim binary in this cycle.

layer_b_tool_idiom.name: --help fast-path 5-line idiom
layer_b_tool_idiom.place: FIRST executable lines of every tool, before ANY exec/read_file/network call
constraints: no exec() except the single printf to read HEXA_ARGV
must return BEFORE any 'let DEFAULT_ROOTS', 'let MEM_ROOT' top-level reads if they trigger I/O
layer_b_tool_idiom.optional_extension: support --help-full for context-rich help (e.g. discovered backends from registry) — gated separately

honest_findings.audit_24_hypothesis_revisited: Hypothesis 'import-time env probes blocking' is REJECTED by evidence. Real cause is binary: (1) shim env-leak, (2) tool-side NO_HELP_GATE. Neither is 'import-time env probes'.
honest_findings.3s_timeout_verdict: ACCEPTABLE — keep audit timeout at 3s; it correctly catches defects.

raw_compliance.raw_66_ai_native_error: n/a (no error trailer needed for read-only audit)
raw_compliance.raw_71_report_only: PASS — no tool modified, no shim patched, no atlas mutated by this cycle
raw_compliance.raw_77_append_only: PASS — auto-ingest will append L0 only
raw_compliance.raw_82_root_fix: PASS — investigation surfaced shim-level env-leak which is itself a raw-82 sub-defect; documented for follow-up kick

kick(ω-cycle) cli_budget_gate-help-gate — add 5-line --help fast-path; this tool is the WORST violator (warning emit + subprocess spawn + disk write on --help)
kick(ω-cycle) atlas_absorb_lint-help-gate — emits witness file on --help; admissibility violation
kick(ω-cycle) container-pid-ulimit-bump OR zombie-reaper — hexa-exec hits 512 PID ceiling under cumulative test load; either bump ulimit (--pids-limit) or add tini-style reaper
broaden bench: re-run #24 audit AFTER shim fix to measure real tool-side defect count (current 76 conflates shim leak + tool gate)
